Petition To Invalidate Trust Amendment
Off Calendar Aug-11-2020 Continued To Nov-10-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Charles M. Collins appearing by CourtCall for Edward Fung (Petitioner). Michael Freedman appearing by CourtCall for Maria S. Matsu (Trustee).

The Court stated before it today is a Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel and Petition to Invalidate Trust Amendment. Mr. Collins stated that the Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel should be taken off calendar and that he had sent in a letter requesting that the motion be taken off calendar. The Court stated that had it seen the letter that it would have continued this matter and entered its Case Management Order. The Court stated that it would issue its Case Management Order Ordering Accounts, Setting Case Management Hearing(s), Ordering Joint Case Management Statement(s), Setting Trial And Ordering Joint Trial Plan; Related Orders. The Court stated that order sets a Case Management Conference on November 10, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and that the order directs counsel to meet and confer 15 days prior to the CMC to participate in a good faith effort to draft a joint case management statement which is to filed 10 days prior to the CMC. The Court stated that the order also sets a trial date of February 16, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court ordered Mr. Collins to serve the Case Management Order on all parties and to look for the uploaded order. Mr. Collins stated that he was looking for extra time to complete discovery. Mr. Freedman stated that this was the first that he had heard that Mr. Collins was taking his Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel off calendar, that he thought that they would be dealing with new counsel, that he wanted the Court to assign the case to pro bono mediation, that this case falls within the three million dollar limit, and that the parties could report back in sixty days which would not disrupt the CMC order. Mr. Collins stated that his client was not in favor of mediation. The Court stated that the Case Management Order talks about ADR and that if the parties come to an agreement regarding the pro bono mediation that they should contact the clerk. The Court stated that prior to trial that it expects that counsel will agree on some form of ADR.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

Petition Of Maria S. Matsu, Trustee Of The Diana M. Lew Trust Dated March 21, 2017 For: 1. Order Confirming Trust Assets And Transferring Assets Into Trust
Off Calendar Feb-18-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Charles Collins for Edward Fung (Petitioner).
Michael Freedman for Maria Matsu.

Counsel indicated that they have talked, but there is no resolution yet, that they need to conduct discovery with regard to the petition to invalidate the trust amendment, and that there is no objection to the 850 Petition. The Court granted the Petition for Order Confirming Trust Assets, etc. (subject to review of the order). The Petition to Invalidate Trust Amendment is continued to May 19, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to meet and confer, and are to file a joint status statement by May 12, 2020 as to how they will move forward.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.
